title: castela calcicola
intro: Castela calcicola is a shrub or small tree species native to the Americas, particularly found in dry and semi-dry regions, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and edible fruits. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions, wounds, and infections due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Castela calcicola include its ability to soothe skin irritations, promote wound healing, and aid in the management of fever. Herbal preparations of Castela calcicola often involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves or roots, which can be used to treat various health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
